$description: Per-screen handoff spec for "Child select" (S4) in flow "xplor-hub-login". Generated by scripts/build-handoff.mjs — do not edit by hand.
name: Child select
purpose: Sign children in — booked sessions plus casual sign-in for children without a booking.
exit: Stays on screen (toast per sign-in); back → S3
layout: Hub shell with back-link 'Dashboard' (returns to Role select); title 'Attendance'
Section 'Bookings today': 2-col grid of child cards (photo, name, session time, 'Booked Session' cyan pill, trailing 'Sign In' link)
Section 'No bookings': child cards with an orange 'Casual Session' pill and a trailing 'Casual Session' link

redlines.spec: 24px; cyan tone for booked, orange tone for casual

behaviour: 'Sign In' on a booked child shows the toast '{name} signed in' and stays on this screen (the action stops propagation — the card itself does not navigate).
Children without a booking carry the orange Casual Session pill and sign in via the 'Casual Session' action, with the same toast.
The back link returns to Role select (S3) despite its 'Dashboard' label — see open questions.
a11y: The toast is visual only — build needs aria-live=polite so sign-ins are announced.
Sign In / Casual Session are text buttons nested in a role=button card — keep them independently focusable with distinct names (e.g. 'Sign in Jessica Jones').

acceptance.text: Booked and no-booking children are visually distinct.
acceptance.assert: booked rows have cyan 'Booked Session' pill + 'Sign In' action; no-booking rows have orange 'Casual Session' pill + 'Casual Session' action

acceptance.text: Signing a child in confirms with a toast and stays on the screen.
acceptance.assert: click('Sign In') -> toast '{name} signed in' visible, auto-dismissed ~1.6s, step == child
